q: Which statement is INCORRECT about insulin?
options: It is released by pancreatic glands.
Diabetic patients produce huge amounts of insulin.
Insulin helps the body break down the glucose in the blood.
Bacteria are used to produce insulin by biotechnology.
skill: Define bacterium and explain how genes are introduced into a bacterium
feedback: Insulin is a hormone, released by pancreatic glands, that helps the body break down glucose in the blood. Currently, it is produced commercially with the help of bacteria using biotechnology. Diabetic patients have higher glucose levels in their blood due to a lack of insulin production.

subject: Science
q: Which of the following chemicals is released by the pancreatic gland?
skill: Define bacterium and explain how genes are introduced into a bacterium
feedback: Insulin is a hormone, released by pancreatic glands, that helps the body break down the glucose in the blood.

subject: Science
q: _____ is a disease where glucose levels in the blood are very high.
skill: Define bacterium and explain how genes are introduced into a bacterium
feedback:
Diabetes is a disease where the glucose levels in the blood are very high.

Constipation is a condition in which a person finds it difficult to pass faeces out of the body. Asthma is a chronic lung disease that inflames and narrows the airways. A common cold is a viral infection in which your nose and throat are affected.

subject: Science
q: Genetic engineering is
options: the creation of buildings by engineers that are good for the environment.
a change in the genes of organisms that produces random features.
a change by humans in the genes of organisms to produce desirable features.
the adaptation of animals to their environments through a change in their genes.
skill: Discuss the benefits of genetic modification in agriculture and the environment
feedback: Genetic engineering, also known as genetic modification, is a change by humans in the genes of organisms to produce desirable features. Genetic engineering is already being used in fields like medicine and agriculture and has the potential to play a large role in improving the quality of life of humans in the future.

subject: Science
q: Suppose a species of rice has one of the following properties. Which property would genetic engineers be MOST interested in?
options: It can remain flooded in water for several weeks without dying.
It attracts all sorts of insects that quickly destroy the crop.
It can only grow properly within a narrow range of temperature.
It quickly absorbs and retains any pesticide or herbicide sprayed on it.
skill: Discuss the benefits of genetic modification in agriculture and the environment
feedback: Farmers lose a large percentage of crops every year because of disease, extreme weather, and lack of proper care. While rice crops normally need to be kept in water to grow properly, most varieties will die if they remain flooded for too long. A species that can remain flooded for several weeks without dying is valuable to genetic engineers. If they succeed in isolating the gene that gives rice this property, other varieties of rice can benefit as well.

subject: Science
q: How are soil pollutants like mercury and trinitrotoluene (TNT) affected by genetically modified plants?
options: The plants absorb the pollutants and remove them from the soil.
The plants change the pollutants into their gaseous state.
The plants make the animals in the area resistant to these pollutants.
The plants produce enzymes that break down the pollutants.
skill: Discuss the benefits of genetic modification in agriculture and the environment
feedback: Enzymes produced by certain types of bacteria can break down soil pollutants into substances that are not harmful. Through genetic modification, the genes that make these enzymes can be inserted into plants. The plants then produce enzymes to break down the pollutants.

subject: Science
q: What is the main benefit of genetically modifying rice so it contains more vitamin A?
options: The extra vitamin A improves the flavour of rice.
The rice has an attractive golden colour and more people buy it.
Vitamin A is good for vision and improves eyesight.
Vitamin A is good for bones as it helps with the absorption of calcium.
skill: Discuss the benefits of genetic modification in agriculture and the environment
feedback:
Genetically modified rice that contains extra vitamin A is known as golden rice. Vitamin A is good for vision and improves eyesight. Golden rice is especially beneficial in countries where the population has a deficiency of vitamin A.

While the rice does have a golden colour, this is not the reason for genetically modifying it.

subject: Science
q: The genetic modification of canola can decrease its bitterness. This is an example of genetic modification that
options: makes the crop resistant to disease.
kills any weeds that steal the crops' nutrients.
skill: Discuss the benefits of genetic modification in agriculture and the environment
feedback: Canola is a type of oilseed that is commonly used as a vegetable oil. Some varieties have a bitter taste that people do not like. Genetic modification can be used to decrease the bitterness of the oil, therefore improving its flavour.

subject: Science
q: How can genetically modified bacteria clean up areas affected by an oil spill?
options: The bacteria increase the resistance of affected plants.
The bacteria produce enzymes that break down the oil.
The bacteria form a protective layer around the oil, so it doesn't spread.
The bacteria seal the source of the oil spill to stop further leakage.
skill: Discuss the benefits of genetic modification in agriculture and the environment
feedback: Oil spills cause widespread environmental damage. They kill and harm the plants and animals in the affected area and are hard to clean up. Their damage can be limited by using genetically modified bacteria that produce enzymes to break down the oil. Once its broken down, the oil is no longer harmful to plants and animals.

subject: Science
q: Farmers add fertilisers to crops to
options: improve the nutritional value of food.
make the crops resistant to disease.
kill any weeds that steal the crops' nutrients.
kill any genetically modified varieties of crops.
skill: Discuss the benefits of genetic modification in agriculture and the environment
feedback:
The quality of a crop depends on the quality of the soil it grows in. Fertilisers increase nutrients in soil. This leads to an improvement in the nutritional value of the food produced by the crop.

Some fertilisers are poisonous to humans. One solution is to use genetically modified varieties of crops that do not need fertilisers.

subject: Science
q:
The steps below show the genetic modification of corn. Choose the correct order for these steps.

I. Scientists insert the gene that produces the poison into corn.
II. Scientists find a bacterium that produces a substance that poisons insects.
III. Scientists discover the gene that produces the poison.
skill: Discuss the benefits of genetic modification in agriculture and the environment
feedback:
The bacterium that produces this poison is known as bacillus thuringiensis. The corn that contains this bacterium's genes is known as Bt corn (Bt is a short form of the bacterium's name). The correct order for the steps is:

II. Scientists find a bacterium that produces a substance that poisons insects.
III. Scientists discover the gene that produces the poison.
I. Scientists insert the gene that produces the poison into corn.

subject: Science
q: Cells that can grow to become any type of cell in the body are known as
skill: Discuss the benefits of genetic modification in health and medicine
feedback: Stem cells are cells that can grow to become any type of cell in the body. They are of special interest to scientists and are being researched to see if they can cure a large variety of diseases.

subject: Science
q: Which invention is an example of biotechnology, but NOT genetic modification?
options: an artificial knee made of a material that does not react with the body
a hormone produced in a lab to treat a deficiency of red blood cells
a vaccine created by injecting a virus into chick cells
skill: Discuss the benefits of genetic modification in health and medicine
feedback: Knees are important joints as they are essential for walking and bear a lot of the body's weight. Knee replacements, especially in old age, are common procedures because the joint can become weakened over time. In this procedure, an artificial knee is used and is made of a material that does not react with the body. The material is normally a combination of metal and plastic. No genes are modified in these artificial knees, so this is NOT an example of genetic modification.

subject: Science
q: What is a vaccine?
options: a substance used to stimulate the production of antibodies
the medium in which the gene of one organism is inserted into another
a cell that can grow to become any type of cell in the body
a substance in the bloodstream that reduces the number of white blood cells
skill: Discuss the benefits of genetic modification in health and medicine
feedback: A vaccine is a substance used to stimulate the production of antibodies. These antibodies are the body's disease-fighting cells and are produced by white blood cells.

subject: Science
q:
The statements below show the first three steps of creating a vaccine for people with measles.

I. The measles virus is taken from a person who has the disease.
II. The virus is placed in the cells of young chicks.
III. The virus weakens over time because of a change in its environment.

What is the next step in this process?
options: The weakened virus is injected into the tissue of cattle.
The gene that weakens the virus is isolated by scientists.
The cells of the young chicks are injected into fully-grown hens.
The weakened virus is injected into the tissue of humans.
skill: Discuss the benefits of genetic modification in health and medicine
feedback: Once the virus has weakened, it is injected into the tissue of humans. The weakened virus stimulates the production of antibodies to guard the body against the measles virus in the future.

subject: Science
q:
The steps below show how insulin is produced by genetically modified bacteria. Place these steps in the correct order.

I. The genetically modified bacterium starts dividing rapidly and makes insulin.
II. The gene responsible for insulin production in human cells is isolated.
III. The gene responsible for insulin production is joined with DNA from a bacterium.
IV. The genetically modified DNA is introduced into another bacterium cell.
skill: Discuss the benefits of genetic modification in health and medicine
feedback:
The technique to make insulin using genetically modified bacteria was discovered in the 1980s. The correct order is:

II. The gene responsible for insulin production in human cells is isolated.
III. The gene responsible for insulin production is joined with DNA from a bacterium.
IV. The genetically modified DNA is introduced into another bacterium cell
I. The genetically modified bacterium starts dividing rapidly and makes insulin.

subject: Science
q: How do doctors treat patients with diabetes in the 21<sup>st</sup> century?
options: They inject patients with insulin taken from animals.
They inject patients with insulin made by bacteria.
They tell patients to eat foods rich in vitamin D.
They vaccinate patients by inserting a weak virus into the body.
skill: Discuss the benefits of genetic modification in health and medicine
feedback: In the 1980s, scientists discovered a way to create pure insulin on a large scale using genetically modified bacteria. The insulin is then injected into the body. This method is effective for treating diabetes and is still used nowadays.

subject: Science
q: How did doctors treat patients with diabetes in the early 20<sup>th</sup> century?
options: They injected patients with insulin taken from animals.
They injected patients with insulin made by yeast.
They injected patients with insulin made by bacteria.
They vaccinated patients by inserting a weak virus into the body.
skill: Discuss the benefits of genetic modification in health and medicine
feedback: Before the 20th century, there was no cure for diabetes. People who had the disease fell seriously ill and often died. In the 1920s, doctors started to inject patients with insulin taken from animals. This procedure still had risks as people suffered from allergic reactions or did not respond to the injections.

subject: Science
q: Which of the following would you use to fill a balloon for your birthday?
skill: Classify elements into metals and non-metals, and relate their physical properties to their uses
feedback: Potassium is a metal so we can't fill the balloon with that. Chlorine may be a non-metal, but it is dangerous for humans to breathe in. Helium and hydrogen are both light non-metals. However, hydrogen is very reactive and might explode, especially near flames. Therefore, the correct answer is helium.

subject: Science
q: At room temperature, except for bromine, non-metals are
skill: Classify elements into metals and non-metals, and relate their physical properties to their uses
feedback: Non-metals, except for bromine, are generally found in solid and gaseous states at room temperature.

subject: Science
q: At room temperature, except for mercury, most metals are found
skill: Classify elements into metals and non-metals, and relate their physical properties to their uses
feedback: Metals are generally solids at room temperature, except for mercury.

subject: Science
q: Elements that sink in water, and start boiling only at high temperatures, are classified as
skill: Classify elements into metals and non-metals, and relate their physical properties to their uses
feedback: Most metals are generally heavier, which is why they sink in water. They also have a higher boiling point, which means they can only be brought to boil if the temperature is high.

subject: Science
q: Which of the following can be beaten into thin sheets?
skill: Classify elements into metals and non-metals, and relate their physical properties to their uses
feedback: Metals are malleable, which means they can be beaten into thin sheets without breaking apart. Zinc is the only metal, whereas the rest are non-metals.

subject: Science
q: Metals are lustrous and shiny in appearance. This means they can reflect
skill: Classify elements into metals and non-metals, and relate their physical properties to their uses
feedback: Metals have reflective surfaces, so they reflect light easily, giving them a shiny appearance.

subject: Science
q: Elements that are brittle and cannot be stretched into wires are classified as
skill: Classify elements into metals and non-metals, and relate their physical properties to their uses
feedback: Elements that are brittle and cannot be stretched into wires have been classified by scientists as non-metals. Metals, on the other hand, are ductile, and can be stretched into wires without breaking.

subject: Science
q: Which metal is found in liquid state at room temperature?
skill: Classify elements into metals and non-metals, and relate their physical properties to their uses
feedback: Hg is mercury. Mercury is the only metal that is found in liquid state at room temperature, which is why we use it in thermometers.

subject: Science
q: Which element does not have the property of malleability?
skill: Classify elements into metals and non-metals, and relate their physical properties to their uses
feedback: Carbon is a non-metal, which is why it is not malleable.

subject: Science
q: Which one of the following is a good conductor of electricity?
skill: Classify elements into metals and non-metals, and relate their physical properties to their uses
feedback: Metals are good conductors of electricity. Iron is a metal.

subject: Science
q: Which element is a non-metal?
skill: Classify elements into metals and non-metals, and relate their physical properties to their uses
feedback: N is for nitrogen. Nitrogen is a non-metal. The others are all metals. Zn is zinc, Mg is magnesium, and Na is sodium.

subject: Science
q: While stirring her porridge over the stove with a spoon, Kulsoom burnt her hand. Why?
options: The spoon was made of silver.
The spoon was made of wood.
The spoon was made of plastic.
The spoon was made of ceramic.
skill: Classify elements into metals and non-metals, and relate their physical properties to their uses
feedback: Silver is a metal, and metals are good conductors of heat.

subject: Science
q: Which element is often used to make wires?
skill: Classify elements into metals and non-metals, and relate their physical properties to their uses
feedback: Metals are ductile, and good conductors of electricity. Although gold and copper are both metals, <b>copper<b> is often used to make wires because it is cheaper than gold.

subject: Science
q: Which element is a metal?
skill: Classify elements into metals and non-metals, and relate their physical properties to their uses
feedback: Na is the symbol for sodium. Sodium is a metal. The others are all non-metals. C is carbon, N is nitrogen, and Cl is chlorine.

subject: Science
q: Scientists have classified elements into which two important groups?
skill: Classify elements into metals and non-metals, and relate their physical properties to their uses
feedback: Elements make up all matter. Scientists have classified these elements as either metals or non-metals.

subject: Science
q: In the chemical reaction, H\[_{2}\] + O\[_{2}\]\[\rightarrow\] H\[_{2}\]O\[_{2}\], the H\[_{2}\]O\[_{2}\] is a
skill: Define and describe chemical reactions with examples - Science
feedback: Products of a chemical reaction are the substances that are formed in the reaction, whereas reactants are the substances that take part in the reaction. Therefore, products are written on the right side of the arrow. Here, H\[_{2}\]O\[_{2}\] is the product.

subject: Science
q: The chemical reaction of sulphur powder with oxygen gas to form sulphur dioxide can be written as
skill: Define and describe chemical reactions with examples - Science
feedback:
Sulphur is an element, so its chemical formula is S. Oxygen gas exists as the molecule O\[_{2}\]. Sulphur dioxide is the molecule SO\[_{2}\]. Therefore, the reaction can be written as:
S + O\[_{2}\]   \[\rightarrow\]   SO\[_{2}\]

subject: Science
q: Which statement is NOT true for chemical reactions?
options: Chemical properties of substances remain unchanged after the reaction.
They are not easy to reverse.
More than one product may be formed.
skill: Define and describe chemical reactions with examples - Science
feedback: A chemical reaction changes the composition of a substance and the new substances formed will have completely different chemical properties, that's why we often say that it is irreversible.

subject: Science
q: A fruit may change colour when it ripens. Which reaction is it undergoing?
skill: Define and describe chemical reactions with examples - Science
feedback: The ripening of fruits, such as bananas or apples, is a chemical reaction. A few reactions take place during the ripening phase. The colour of the fruit changes, as does its texture. The fruit becomes soft with the breakdown of its constituents. The sugar content of the fruit changes, and the fruit becomes sweeter. Also, we cannot change back the fruit to its original state after it changes.

subject: Science
q:
Identify the product in the given chemical reaction.

C + O\[_{2}\]  \[\rightarrow\]   CO\[_{2}\]
skill: Define and describe chemical reactions with examples - Science
feedback: Products of a chemical reaction are the substances that are formed in the reaction, whereas reactants are the substances that take part in the reaction. Therefore, products are written on the right side of the arrow. Here, CO\[_{2}\] is the product.

subject: Science
q:
Identify the reactants in the given chemical reaction.

C + O\[_{2}\]  \[\rightarrow\]   CO\[_{2}\]
skill: Define and describe chemical reactions with examples - Science
feedback: Reactants are substances that combine and make new substances in a chemical reaction, and they are always written on the left side of the arrow in a chemical equation. However, products that are formed are written on the right side of the arrow.

subject: Science
q: The chemical reaction of carbon powder with oxygen gas to form carbon dioxide can be written as
skill: Define and describe chemical reactions with examples - Science
feedback:
Carbon is an element, so its chemical formula is C. Oxygen gas exists as the molecule O\[_{2}\]. Carbon dioxide is the molecule CO\[_{2}\]. Therefore, the reaction can be written as:
C + O\[_{2}\]  \[\rightarrow\]   CO\[_{2}\]

subject: Science
q: The reactions which absorb energy from their surroundings, usually in the form of heat are the _____.
skill: Explain the energy changes in chemical reactions - Science
feedback: The chemical reactions in which energy is absorbed are called endothermic reactions. Most of the time, this energy is absorbed in the form of heat energy.

subject: Science
q: Which one of the following is an endothermic reaction?
options: the burning of a match stick
the cooking of an egg
the formation of snow in the clouds
skill: Explain the energy changes in chemical reactions - Science
feedback:
Cooking of an egg is an example of an endothermic reaction. Egg absorbs the heat that allows it to cook.
Other reactions such as the burning of a match stick, the formation of snow in the clouds, and the burning of sugar give out heat energy, thus, they are exothermic.

subject: Science
q:
Why is this reaction exothermic?
H2 + Cl2 → 2HCl
options: More bonds are formed than broken.
More bonds are broken than formed.
More energy is absorbed during bond breaking than released during bond formation.
Less energy is absorbed during bond breaking than released during bond formation.
skill: Explain the energy changes in chemical reactions - Science
feedback: In an exothermic reaction, the energy involved in bond formation is always greater than the energy involved in the breaking of bonds. All exothermic reactions have energy released as a bi-product.

subject: Science
q:
This chemical equation shows an endothermic reaction. Which statement is true about this chemical reaction?
H2 + I2 → 2HI
options: The number of new bonds formed is greater than the number of bonds broken.
The formation of H-I bonds releases energy.
The total energy change in bond formation is less than that in bond breaking.
The products possess less energy than the reactants.
skill: Explain the energy changes in chemical reactions - Science
feedback:
In an endothermic reaction, energy is absorbed by the reactants to form the product.
In this reaction, the number of bonds formed are equal to the number of bonds broken.
Bond formation releases energy, while bond breaking absorbs energy. Since this is an endothermic reaction, energy is not being released, but rather absorbed to form new bonds.
In an endothermic reaction, the energy of the product is higher than the energy of the reactants. Therefore, in endothermic reactions, energy change during bond breaking is larger than during bond formation.
In endothermic reactions, energy is absorbed from the surroundings. Therefore, the products have higher energy than reactants.

subject: Science
q: Which of the following is an exothermic reaction?
skill: Explain the energy changes in chemical reactions - Science
feedback:
Ice melts because heat is absorbed from the surroundings. When ammonium salts dissolve, the heat is absorbed to break the bonds in the salt. Photosynthesis is a process that stores heat energy from the sun. Therefore, these three reactions are endothermic reactions.
Neutralisation, however, is an exothermic reaction because a base and acid react to produce salt, water, and energy. Energy is released to the surroundings during the process of neutralisation.

subject: Science
q: Reactions that release energy are known as _____.
skill: Explain the energy changes in chemical reactions - Science
feedback: In an exothermic reaction, energy is released. Expressed in the form of a chemical equation: reactants &#8594 products + energy.

subject: Science
q: Baking a cake, photosynthesis, and cooking an egg are chemical reactions. Which statement is true about these reactions?
options: These reactions absorb and release energy.
skill: Explain the energy changes in chemical reactions - Science
feedback: Baking a cake, photosynthesis, and cooking an egg all require heat energy. These processes use heat from their surroundings to form new products. Such reactions are known as endothermic reactions.

subject: Science
q: In this reaction, how can the atoms of magnesium oxide and carbon dioxide be rearranged to form magnesium carbonate?
skill: Explain the types of chemical reactions with examples - Science
feedback:
The figure represents an addition reaction where magnesium oxide (MgO) combines with carbon dioxide (CO2) to form magnesium carbonate. The chemical formula of magnesium carbonate is MgCO3.
In an addition reaction, two or more substances combine to form a single product.

subject: Science
q: If NH3 is added to HCl, then the product will be _____.
skill: Explain the types of chemical reactions with examples - Science
feedback:
This is an addition reaction, where NH3 is added to HCl and produces NH4Cl. The chemical equation for this reaction can be written as:
NH3 + HCl → NH4Cl

subject: Science
q: A balanced chemical equation for the decomposition reaction of KClO<sub>3</sub> can be expressed as:
skill: Explain the types of chemical reactions with examples - Science
feedback: To balance a chemical equation, the number of atoms on both sides of a chemical equation should be equal. 2 molecules of KClO<sub>3</sub> are broken down to form these components; 2 molecules of KCl and 3 molecules of O<sub>2</sub>.

subject: Science
q: If 2 molecules of sodium hydroxide (NaOH) decompose, what will the product(s) be?
skill: Explain the types of chemical reactions with examples - Science
feedback:
The balanced chemical equation for the decomposition reaction of 2 molecules of sodium hydroxide is:
2NaOH → Na2O + H2O

subject: Science
q: What products do we get if hydrogen carbonate (H<sub>2</sub>CO<sub>3</sub>) decomposes into its components, while the equation remains balanced?
skill: Explain the types of chemical reactions with examples - Science
feedback:
The balanced chemical equation for the decomposition of hydrogen carbonate (H2CO3) can be expressed as:
H2CO3 → CO2 + H2O
The number of atoms on both sides are equal and the products are the exact components of H2CO3.

subject: Science
q: What is the product of this addition reaction?
skill: Explain the types of chemical reactions with examples - Science
feedback: In the above reaction, 2 atoms of sodium (Na) combine with 2 molecules of chlorine (Cl<sub>2</sub>) to form sodium chloride (NaCl). Chemical equations must be balanced, as the number of atoms used as reactants should be the same for the products.

subject: Science
q: This reaction is an example of _____
skill: Explain the types of chemical reactions with examples - Science
feedback: In the above reaction, calcium carbonate (CaCO<sub>3</sub>) splits into its components, calcium oxide (CaO) and carbon dioxide (CO<sub>2</sub>). Therefore, it is a decomposition reaction, as it shows the breakdown of a compound into simpler substances.

subject: Science

feedback: The reactants, vinegar and milk, are mixed to form the product, curd. The mass of the products is always equal to the mass of the reactants. Therefore, 10g + 80g = 90g

Therefore, the masses of all the reactants is summed up to calculate the mass of the product, that is: 50g + 200g + 5g = 255g

subject: Science
q:
In the reaction,

skill: Define the law of conservation of mass - Science
feedback: According to the law of conservation of mass, the mass of the products always equals the mass of the reactants. The mass of reactants in this equation is 25 g. The mass of one of the products is 10 g. Therefore, the mass of the other product can be calculated by subtracting the masses of the existing reactants and products: 25 - 10 = 15 g. 15 g is the correct answer.

subject: Science
q: In a chemical reaction, 300 g of reactant A react with 100 g of reactant B. How much will the product weigh?
skill: Define the law of conservation of mass - Science
feedback: According to the law of conservation of mass, the mass of the products is always equal to mass of the reactants. Here, the mass of the product will be the sum of the masses of A and B, that is 300 g + 100 g = 400 g. 400 g is the correct answer.

subject: Science
q: The law of conservation of mass implies

the mass of the reactants is less if there is only one product.
the mass of the reactants is equal to the mass of the products.
the mass of the products is greater than that of the reactants.

feedback: According to the law of conservation of mass, matter can neither be created nor destroyed during a chemical reaction but it may undergo changes in shape and state. This means that regardless of what form it takes, the amount of matter will remain the same before and after a chemical reaction.

q: The process of rusting is an exothermic reaction because

skill: Describe the importance of exothermic reactions in daily life - Science
feedback: The process of rusting involves a reaction between iron and oxygen. It is an exothermic process as it involves the combustion of iron in air. Therefore, rusting is exothermic as it generates a lot of heat. Unfortunately, this particular reaction takes place so slowly that the release of heat is undetectable.

subject: Science
q: Motorised vehicles are propelled as energy is provided by the combustion of gasoline. What type of reaction is taking place?
skill: Describe the importance of exothermic reactions in daily life - Science
feedback: Combustion reactions are always exothermic as combustion gives out a lot of heat. Burning gasoline involves combustion and releases heat, therefore, it is an exothermic reaction.

subject: Science
q: When laundry detergent gets wet, it warms up because
options: chemical bond energy is converted into thermal energy.
oxygen gas is released as a result of the reaction between water and detergent.
a large amount of energy is absorbed in the reaction.
the bonds between the particles of the detergent are broken.
skill: Describe the importance of exothermic reactions in daily life - Science
feedback: An exothermic reaction is one that produces heat as chemical bond energy is converted into thermal energy. The chemical reaction takes place between the detergent and the water, thus producing a gas as a by-product in the process. This gas begins to escape the mixture as bubbles appear, thus heating the mixture.

subject: Science
q:
Which examples represent exothermic reactions?

I.    photosynthesis
II.  boiling of water
III. neutralization reaction
IV. the formation of snow in the clouds
skill: Describe the importance of exothermic reactions in daily life - Science
feedback:
The reactions which give out heat in the surroundings are called exothermic reactions. The burning of a match gives out heat, therefore, it is an exothermic reaction.
Photosynthesis involves the absorption of light and heat energy from the sun. Therefore, it is an endothermic reaction.
When water boils, it absorbs heat energy. So, boiling of water is an endothermic reaction too.
However, neutralisation is an exothermic reaction because a base and acid react to produce salt, water, and energy. Energy is released to the surroundings during the process of neutralisation. Thus, it is an exothermic reaction.
The formation of snow in the clouds involves bond formation. Thus, it is also an exothermic reaction.

subject: Science
q: The energy given off in an exothermic chemical reaction is usually the result of forming _____.
skill: Describe the importance of exothermic reactions in daily life - Science
feedback: Energy is given off when bonds are formed. During exothermic reactions, the energy released in bond formation is greater than the energy released in bond breaking.

subject: Science
q: The crystallisation of liquid salts is an exothermic reaction because
options: the motion between the particles of salt is increased.
the motion between the particles of salt is reduced.
the bonds between the particles of salt are broken.
the particles of salt are chemically binding with water.
skill: Describe the importance of exothermic reactions in daily life - Science
feedback: Matter stores energy (kinetic energy) in the form of motion of its constituent particles (atoms or molecules). Whenever crystallisation takes place, it involves a reduction in the motion of these constituent particles, which is given out in the form of heat. Therefore, it is called an exothermic change.

subject: Science
q: The body's ability to use glucose for performing functions and producing heat indicates that
options: glucose is only present in foods that are hot.
skill: Describe the importance of exothermic reactions in daily life - Science
feedback: We consume food containing glucose, which is a simpler form of carbohydrates. Carbohydrates are broken down into simpler substances like glucose, which is further broken down into water and carbon dioxide and releases energy. This energy is released into our cells, thus allowing our bodies to function.

subject: Science

Therefore, it gains one more electron to form a \[\ce{Cl^{-}}\] ion.

subject: Science
q: Which of the following is the correct dot-and-cross diagram of ionic bonding in beryllium fluoride?

feedback: Beryllium fluoride (\[\ce{BeF2}\]) is made from one beryllium \[\ce{Be^{+2}}\] ion and two fluoride (\[\ce{F^{-}}\]) ions. \[\ce{Be}\] contains 4 electrons, but it gives up its two valence electron in its second shell to form a \[\ce{Be^{+2}}\] ion. \[\ce{F}\] contains 9 electrons, but 7 of those are valence electrons in its second shell. Therefore, it gains one more electron to form a \[\ce{F^{-}}\] ion and complete its octet.

subject: Science
q: Which of the following is the correct dot-and-cross diagram of ionic bonding in lithium oxide?

feedback: Lithium oxide (\[\ce{Li2O}\]) is made from two lithium \[\ce{Li+}\] ions and one oxide (\[\ce{O^{-2}}\]) ion. \[\ce{Li}\] contains 3 electrons, but it gives up the one valence electron in its second shell to form a \[\ce{Li+}\] ion. \[\ce{O}\] contains 8 electrons, but 6 valence electrons in its second shell. Therefore, it gains two more electrons to form a \[\ce{O^{-2}}\] ion and completes its octet.

subject: Science
q: Which of the following is the correct dot-and-cross diagram of ionic bonding in sodium sulphide?

Therefore, it gains two more electrons to form a \[\ce{S^{-2}}\] ion and complete its octet.

subject: Science
q: To form ionic bond between two atoms, one loses an electron to become a ______ and the other gains an electron to become a ______.
skill: Describe the formation of ionic bonds and draw dot and cross structures of ionic compounds
feedback: The atom which loses an electron, becomes positively charged and it is called "cation."  The atom which gains an electron becomes negatively charged and it is called "anion."  This opposite force of attraction holds the ions together and it is called "ionic bond." After gaining or losing an electron, the atom is no longer neutral since it has either excess or deficiency of negative charge, respectively.

subject: Science
q: Which of the following is an example of an ionic compound?
skill: Describe the formation of ionic bonds and draw dot and cross structures of ionic compounds
feedback:
Magnesium (Mg) is an alkaline earth metal, and Oxygen (O) is a non-metal. A Mg atom has two electrons in its outermost shell. It loses these two electrons to form a Mg+2 ion. An O atom has six electrons in its outermost shell. O gains the two electrons donated by Mg to form a O-2 ion. This exchange of electrons is an example of ionic bonding. The resulting ionic compound is magnesium oxide with the formula MgO.

Other compounds, NH3, MgO, and CH4, are covalent compounds because C is sharing its 4 electrons with 4 Cl atoms in CCl4 and with 4 H atoms in CH4. N is sharing its 3 electrons with 3 H atoms in NH3.

subject: Science
q: In the image given below, the chlorine atoms will each gain _____ electron(s) from the magnesium atom.
skill: Describe the formation of ionic bonds and draw dot and cross structures of ionic compounds
feedback:
Magnesium (Mg) is in group II of the periodic table and has two electrons in its valence shell. The Mg atom will lose these two electrons to complete an octet and form a Mg+2 ion.
On the other hand, chlorine (Cl) is a group VII element. Its atom will gain one electron to complete an octet and form a stable Cl-1 ion.
As the single Mg atom loses two electrons, the two Cl atoms gain one electron each. In this example, the electrons are shown as dots and crosses.

Two chlorine atoms will each gain one electron from the magnesium atoms. The atom becomes ions.

subject: Science
q: Which statement best describes the image given below?
options: Mg gains two electrons to form a Mg<sup>-2</sup> ion.
Mg gains two electrons to form a Mg<sup>+2</sup> ion.
Mg loses its two electrons to form a Mg<sup>-2</sup> ion.
Mg loses its two electrons to form a Mg<sup>+2</sup> ion.

feedback:
Mg is a metal, and F is a non-metal. A Mg atom has two electrons in its outermost shell as it is in Group 2. It loses these two electrons to form a Mg+2 ion. Alternatively, the F atom has seven electrons in its outermost shell as it is in Group 7. It gains one electron to form a F- ion.
For every Mg+2 ion, two F- ions are needed.

subject: Science
q: Which of the following atoms will lose an electron(s) during the formation of the ionic compound, NaCl?
skill: Describe the formation of ionic bonds and draw dot and cross structures of ionic compounds
feedback: Na is a metal, and Cl is a non-metal. Metal atoms tend to lose electrons while non-metal atoms tend to gain electrons, to acquire the electronic configuration of their nearest noble gas. A Na atom has one electron in the outer most shell, it loses this electron to form a Na+ ion. A Cl atom has seven electrons in its outermost shell, it needs one electron to complete its octet. The Cl atom gains one electron from Na to form a Cl- ion. The chemical formula of the resulting compound is NaCl.

subject: Science
q: Ionic bonding is a type of bonding that involves the electrostatic attraction between _____.
skill: Describe the formation of ionic bonds and draw dot and cross structures of ionic compounds
feedback: Ionic bonding is the transfer of valence electron(s) from one atom (usually metal) to another (usually non-metal). Ionic bonding usually occurs when a metal loses electrons to become a positively charged cation, while a non-metal accepts those electrons to become a negatively charged anion. The electrostatic attraction between these two oppositely charged ions results in ionic bonding.

subject: Science
q: The formation of an ionic bond between two atoms is due to ______.
skill: Describe the formation of ionic bonds and draw dot and cross structures of ionic compounds
feedback: Ionic compounds contain positively and negatively charged ions in a ratio that results in an overall charge of zero. Opposite charges attract each other while like charges repel each other. Different charges between the ions are held together in a regular spatial arrangement by electrostatic forces.

subject: Science
q: How many electrons are transferred by each of the atoms of the following elements (calcium, zinc, nitrogen and iodine) when they form ionic compounds?
skill: Describe the formation of ionic bonds and draw dot and cross structures of ionic compounds

Zinc will also lose two electrons to from the Z+2 cation. Nitrogen, being in group VA, will gain three electrons to form N-3 ion to complete its octet since it has only 5 valence electrons. Iodine, being in group VIIA, will gain one electron to complete its octet since it has 7 valence electrons. So, the correct option is A.

subject: Science
q: Which of the following have the same electronic configuration as the ions formed when potassium and chlorine react to form the compound potassium chloride?

subject: Science
q: Identify the correct dot-and-cross diagram of hydrogen sulphide (\[\ce{H2S}\]).

feedback:
Sulphur has 6 valence electrons, so it needs only 2 more to complete its octet.
Hydrogen has 1 valence electron in its first shell, so it needs one more to complete its duplet.
Therefore, sulphur will share only 2 of its valence electrons with two hydrogen atoms, forming 2 single covalent bonds. Since, four electrons in sulphur did not participate in bonding, they will exist as two lone pairs.

subject: Science
q: Identify the correct dot-and-cross diagram of oxygen difluoride (\[\ce{OF2}\]).

Fluorine has 7 valence electrons, so it needs only 1 more to complete its octet.
Therefore, oxygen will share only 2 of its valence electrons with two fluorine atoms, forming 2 single covalent bonds. Since, four electrons in oxygen and six electrons in fluorine did not participate in bonding, they will exist as two and three lone pairs, respectively.

subject: Science
q: Identify the correct dot-and-cross diagram of phosphorus tribromide (\[\ce{PBr3}\]).

Bromine has 7 valence electrons, so it needs only 1 more to complete its octet.
Therefore, phosphorus will share only 3 of its valence electrons with 3 bromine atoms, forming 3 single covalent bonds. Since, two electrons in phosphorus and six electrons in bromine did not participate in bonding, they will exist as one and three lone pairs, respectively.

subject: Science
q: Identify the correct dot-and-cross diagram of chloromethane (\[\ce{CH3Cl}\]).

Hydrogen has 1 valence electron in its first shell, so it needs one more to complete its duplet.
Chlorine has seven valence electrons, so it needs only one more to complete its octet.

Therefore, carbon will share its four electrons with three atoms of hydrogen and one atom of chlorine. Since chlorine has 7 valence electrons but only one is participating in covalent bonding, the remaining six electrons will exist as three lone pairs.

subject: Science
q: Identify the correct dot-and-cross diagram of nitrogen (\[\ce{N2}\]).

feedback: Nitrogen has 5 valence electrons. Therefore, it needs 3 more to complete its octet. Hence, two nitrogen atoms will share their three electrons with each other, forming a triple covalent bond as shown below. Since only three out of five valence electrons are forming bonds, each nitrogen atom will also contain one lone-pair.

subject: Science
q: Identify the correct dot-and-cross diagram of carbon dioxide (\[\ce{CO2}\]).
skill: Describe covalent bonds - Science
feedback: Carbon has four valence electrons and oxygen has six valence electrons. Therefore, carbon needs four more to complete its octet and oxygen needs two. Hence, each carbon atom will share its four electrons with two oxygen atoms, forming two double covalent bonds as shown below. Since only two out of six valence electrons of each oxygen atom are participating in bonding, the remaining four will exist as two lone pairs.

subject: Science
q: This diagram shows a molecule that is formed from 2 different elements covalently bonded together. What are the possible electronic configurations for these elements?
skill: Describe covalent bonds - Science
feedback:
When atoms bond, they achieve a stable electronic configuration where the outermost shell is filled with either 2, or 8, electrons. In this molecule, one blue atom (representing element 1) is covalently bonded to 3 green atoms (representing element 2).
Since the blue atom needs to bond with 3 green atoms to complete its valence shell, this suggests that it has 5 valence electrons, such that it needs 3 more electrons achieve 8 valence electrons. So, a possible configuration is 2.5.
Since each green atom needs to bond with just 1 blue atom to complete its valence shell, this suggests that each green atom has 7 valences electrons, such that it needs 1 more electron to achieve 8 valence electrons. So, a possible configuration is 2.8.7.

subject: Science
q: In hydrogen gas, _____ hydrogen atoms share their single valence electron to form a diatomic molecule.

feedback: A covalent bond is formed by mutual sharing of electrons between two atoms. For example, hydrogen gas is made from hydrogen atoms covalently bonded together. A hydrogen atom has a single valence electron. Two hydrogen atoms share their valence electrons to form a diatomic molecule.

subject: Science
